questions.prompt: What should the first milestone primarily optimize for?
questions.description: The memo proposes a processing system, not another isolated feature island.
questions.recommendedAnswer: A local processing core: artifact model, relations, review workflow, and search should become the center of the app before adding any larger new surface area.

questions.prompt: What should be in scope for the first implementation pass?
questions.description: The memo names a lot of pieces; this pins down the version 0.2 boundary.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Backend modularization, Artifact + Relation model, review queue with K/R/A, SQLite FTS5 search and saved searches, decision/project modules, job detail view, and the existing optional AI hooks.

questions.prompt: What should stay out of scope for now?
questions.description: The memo explicitly warns against overreaching into sync and platform work too early.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Cloud sync, CRDTs, mobile, multiuser, large AI-agent automation, and broad connector rewrites stay out of scope for this pass.

questions.prompt: What should count as success for this version?
questions.description: This should be something we can verify directly in code and behavior.
questions.recommendedAnswer: The app has a modular backend, a shared artifact/relations core, searchable local data with FTS5, a review queue that drives action, and the core workflow still works offline without AI.
